title: Completeness of the Klein-Gordon oscillator eigenfunctions via Hermite and Laguerre polynomials
abstract: Completeness of the Klein--Gordon oscillator eigenfunctions is proved in one and three spatial dimensions. The proofs establish the closure relations satisfied by the eigenfunctions and are based on standard properties of the Hermite and the generalized Laguerre polynomials, supplemented in three dimensions by the completeness of the spherical harmonics. The scalar nature of the Klein--Gordon field renders the argument strictly simpler than the analogous proof for the Dirac oscillator: no off-diagonal cancellation is required.
summary: This paper provides the rigorous mathematical proof that the eigenfunctions of the Klein-Gordon oscillator are complete in one and three dimensions. By linking the KGO to the properties of Hermite and Laguerre polynomials, it justifies the use of spectral representations for Green's functions and partition functions in relativistic scalar models.
main_problem: The paper aims to establish the formal completeness (closure relations) of the Klein-Gordon oscillator (KGO) eigenfunctions in one and three spatial dimensions, filling a gap in the literature where such proofs existed for the Dirac oscillator but not the KGO.
main_result: The author proves that the KGO eigenfunctions form a complete orthonormal set in L2(R) and L2(R3) by reducing the problem to the known closure relations of Hermite and generalized Laguerre polynomials.
method: A constructive proof approach is used, employing separation of variables, reduction to the confluent hypergeometric equation, and the application of properties of special functions and spherical harmonics.
model_or_system: The system is the Klein-Gordon oscillator, a relativistic generalization of the harmonic oscillator for spin-0 particles, where the potential is introduced via minimal substitution p -> p - imw r.
key_observables: Energy eigenvalues (E_n^2 and E_N^2) and the energy spectrum degeneracy.
important_parameters: Mass (m), oscillator frequency (w), and the non-relativistic (m -> infinity) and massless (m -> 0) limits.
main_assumptions: The particle is treated as a scalar field; the proof is limited to 1D and 3D spatial dimensions and does not cover 2D or curved spacetimes.
paper_structure: The paper presents a mathematical proof for 1D completeness using Hermite polynomials, followed by a 3D proof using radial Laguerre polynomials and angular spherical harmonics, concluding with a comparison to the Dirac oscillator and discussion of future extensions.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
